All seven (7) directors then in office were present, constituting a quorum pursuant to Article III of the Bylaws.

**Also Present:**

> • Linda Yeung, Chief Financial Officer and recording secretary
>
> • Nathaniel Rourke, Esq., Avery, Pollard & Glenn LLP, outside counsel

**Board Observer:** A representative of Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP was present in an observer capacity.

**[Approval of Prior Minutes]{.underline}**

The minutes of the regular meeting of the Board held on April 12, 2023, were presented for review. A motion to approve was made by Mr. Trask and seconded by Mr. Garrett. The minutes were unanimously approved.

**[Quarterly Financial Review --- Q2 2023]{.underline}**

Ms. Yeung presented the Company\'s financial results for the second quarter of fiscal year 2023 (\"Q2 2023\"). Revenue for Q2 2023 was \$31,200,000, reflecting an annualized run rate of approximately \$124,800,000. Gross margin was 68%. Operating expenses were on budget across all departments. Ms. Yeung noted that the Company was tracking toward the upper end of FY2023 revenue guidance of \$120,000,000 to \$130,000,000, driven by strong performance in the Permian Basin Energy Co. engagement and continued growth in the Company\'s recurring revenue base. The Company\'s cash and cash equivalents were approximately \$24,000,000 as of the end of Q2 2023. Headcount stood at 398 employees.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby accepts the Q2 2023 financial report as presented by the Chief Financial Officer.

Motion by Mr. Garrett, seconded by Mr. Kleinhans. The resolution was unanimously approved.

**[Special Committee Update --- Strategic Alternatives]{.underline}**

Ms. Lau, Chair of the Special Committee, reported on the status of the strategic alternatives process. Meridian Partners had conducted preliminary diligence sessions with all three potential acquirers previously identified. Ms. Lau reported that two of the three parties --- code names \"Alpha\" and \"Gamma\" --- had either withdrawn from the process or reduced their indications of interest to valuation levels that the Special Committee deemed inadequate. Discussions with the remaining interested party (code name \"Beta\") were continuing, and Beta had expressed a willingness to proceed with confirmatory due diligence. The Special Committee recommended that the Board authorize the Company to enter into an exclusivity period with \"Beta\" for a period of 45 days to allow for focused diligence and negotiation of preliminary terms.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby authorizes the Company, acting through the Chief Executive Officer, to enter into an exclusivity agreement with the remaining interested party (code name \"Beta\") for a period of up to forty-five (45) days, on terms approved by the Special Committee.

Motion by Ms. Lau, seconded by Ms. Okafor. The resolution was approved 6-1. Mr. Trask voted against the resolution, stating that he believed the process had been insufficiently broad and that Stonemill Capital Partners would prefer a wider auction involving additional potential acquirers before the Company committed to an exclusivity arrangement with a single party.

**[Amendment to 2021 Equity Incentive Plan --- Share Reserve Increase]{.underline}**

Ms. Lau, in her capacity as Chair of the Compensation Committee, presented a proposal to amend the Company\'s 2021 Equity Incentive Plan (the \"Plan\") to increase the share reserve available for issuance under the Plan. Ms. Lau explained that the existing share reserve of 8,000,000 shares was substantially allocated, with approximately 7,420,000 shares subject to outstanding awards or previously exercised, leaving fewer than 580,000 shares available for future grants. Ms. Lau stated that additional shares were needed to attract and retain key engineering and management talent as the Company continued to scale its operations and pursue growth opportunities, including the Permian Basin engagement and the ongoing strategic alternatives process.

The Compensation Committee recommended increasing the share reserve by 1,500,000 shares, from the current 8,000,000 shares to 9,500,000 shares. The Board discussed the dilutive impact of the proposed increase. Ms. Yeung presented an analysis demonstrating that the proposed increase would raise the fully diluted option pool from approximately 16.7% to approximately 20.1% of the Company\'s fully diluted capitalization, which the Compensation Committee believed to be within the range of market benchmarks for companies at a comparable stage and size. Outside counsel Rourke noted that the amendment required Board approval.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby approves the amendment to the Company\'s 2021 Equity Incentive Plan to increase the share reserve by 1,500,000 shares, from 8,000,000 shares to 9,500,000 shares, effective as of the date hereof, and the officers of the Company are hereby authorized to take all actions necessary to effectuate such amendment.

Motion by Ms. Lau, seconded by Ms. Chandrasekaran. The resolution was approved (7-0).

**[Other Business and Adjournment]{.underline}**

No other business was raised for discussion. The next regular meeting of the Board was scheduled for October 2023. There being no further business, the meeting was adjourned at 12:45 p.m. Central Time.

Linda Yeung Secretary

**[Meeting 4: Minutes of Regular Meeting of the Board of Directors --- October 11, 2023]{.underline}**

**[Heading and Attendance]{.underline}**

A regular meeting of the Board of Directors of Terravox Systems, Inc. was held on **October 11, 2023**, commencing at **10:00 a.m. Central Time**, at Conference Room A, 4800 Mueller Boulevard, Suite 300, Austin, TX 78723, with the option for attendance via videoconference.

**Board Observer:** A representative of Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP was present in an observer capacity.

**[Approval of Prior Minutes]{.underline}**

The minutes of the regular meeting of the Board held on July 19, 2023, were presented for review. A motion to approve was made by Ms. Okafor and seconded by Ms. Lau. The minutes were unanimously approved.

**[Quarterly Financial Review --- Q3 2023]{.underline}**

Ms. Yeung presented the Company\'s financial results for the third quarter of fiscal year 2023 (\"Q3 2023\"). Revenue for Q3 2023 was \$33,100,000. Year-to-date revenue through the end of Q3 2023 was \$96,400,000. Ms. Yeung confirmed that the Company was on track to achieve FY2023 revenue of approximately \$127,000,000, consistent with the upper end of the previously communicated guidance range. Adjusted EBITDA margin was tracking at approximately 25%. The Company\'s cash position was approximately \$22,000,000 as of the end of Q3 2023. Headcount stood at 408 employees.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby approves and accepts the Q3 2023 financial report as presented by the Chief Financial Officer.

Motion by Mr. Garrett, seconded by Mr. Kleinhans. The resolution was unanimously approved.

**[Strategic Alternatives Update]{.underline}**

Ms. Lau, Chair of the Special Committee, reported that the exclusivity period with the remaining potential acquirer (code name \"Beta\") had been terminated following Beta\'s inability to secure financing commitments on terms acceptable to the Special Committee. Ms. Lau stated that the Special Committee recommended that the strategic alternatives process be paused at this time but that Meridian Partners LLC be retained on a standby basis so that the process could be reactivated promptly should favorable conditions reemerge or should an inbound expression of interest be received. The Board discussed the recommendation and concurred with the Special Committee\'s approach.

**[Clearfield National Bank Term Loan]{.underline}**

CEO Ellery and CFO Yeung presented a proposal for the Company to enter into a term loan facility with Clearfield National Bank, N.A. (the \"Lender\"). The proposed facility would be in the aggregate principal amount of \$12,000,000, secured by substantially all of the Company\'s assets, including accounts receivable, inventory, equipment, and intellectual property. The material terms of the proposed facility included a five (5) year term, with interest at the Secured Overnight Financing Rate (\"SOFR\") plus 3.50% per annum, quarterly principal amortization payments, and customary financial covenants including a minimum debt service coverage ratio and a maximum leverage ratio.

Mr. Ellery explained that the proceeds of the term loan would be used for working capital, capital expenditures related to the Company\'s next-generation sensor platform development program, and general corporate purposes. Ms. Yeung noted that the Company\'s existing cash position of approximately \$22,000,000 was sufficient for near-term operating needs but that the term loan facility would provide additional financial flexibility to support the Company\'s growth investments without constraining the operating budget. Ms. Yeung reviewed the projected debt service requirements and confirmed that the Company\'s projected cash flows were sufficient to service the proposed indebtedness.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby approves the Company\'s entry into a secured term loan facility with Clearfield National Bank, N.A. in the aggregate principal amount of \$12,000,000, on terms substantially consistent with those presented to the Board at this meeting, and hereby authorizes the Chief Executive Officer and the Chief Financial Officer, each acting individually, to negotiate, execute, and deliver on behalf of the Company the definitive loan agreement, promissory note, security agreement, intellectual property security agreement, and all other ancillary documents and instruments as may be required in connection with such facility.

Motion by Mr. Ellery, seconded by Ms. Chandrasekaran. The resolution was approved (7-0).

**[Retention of Outside Counsel]{.underline}**

The Board discussed the engagement of outside counsel for the Company\'s ongoing general corporate matters. Mr. Rourke summarized the scope of services provided by Avery, Pollard & Glenn LLP, including general corporate advisory, employment law matters, commercial contract review, and regulatory compliance. The Board expressed satisfaction with the firm\'s performance and service.

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby approves the continued engagement of Avery, Pollard & Glenn LLP, with Nathaniel Rourke as lead engagement partner, as outside counsel for the Company\'s general corporate, employment, and commercial legal matters, on terms consistent with the firm\'s current engagement letter.

Motion by Ms. Lau, seconded by Mr. Garrett. The resolution was unanimously approved.

**[Other Business and Adjournment]{.underline}**

No other business was raised for discussion. The next regular meeting of the Board was scheduled for January 2024. There being no further business, the meeting was adjourned at 12:30 p.m. Central Time.

Linda Yeung Secretary

**[Meeting 5: Minutes of Regular Meeting of the Board of Directors --- January 24, 2024]{.underline}**

**[Heading and Attendance]{.underline}**

A regular meeting of the Board of Directors of Terravox Systems, Inc. was held on **January 24, 2024**, commencing at **10:00 a.m. Central Time**, at Conference Room A, 4800 Mueller Boulevard, Suite 300, Austin, TX 78723, with the option for attendance via videoconference.

**Directors Present:**

> • Marcus Ellery, Chairman
>
> • Priya Chandrasekaran
>
> • David Kleinhans
>
> • Sandra Okafor
>
> • Julian Trask
>
> • Rebecca Lau
>
> • Thomas Garrett

All seven (7) directors then in office were present, constituting a quorum pursuant to Article III of the Bylaws.

**Also Present:**

> • Linda Yeung, Chief Financial Officer and recording secretary
>
> • Nathaniel Rourke, Esq., Avery, Pollard & Glenn LLP, outside counsel
>
> • A representative of Bancroft Keene & Associates LLP, the Company\'s independent auditors

**Board Observer:** A representative of Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP was present in an observer capacity.

**[Approval of Prior Minutes]{.underline}**

The minutes of the regular meeting of the Board held on October 11, 2023, were presented for review. A motion to approve was made by Mr. Trask and seconded by Ms. Okafor. The minutes were unanimously approved.

**[FY2024 Operating Budget]{.underline}**

Ms. Yeung presented the proposed annual operating budget for fiscal year 2024 (\"FY2024\"). Key highlights of the proposed budget included the following: projected revenue of \$140,000,000 to \$155,000,000; projected Adjusted EBITDA margin of 25% to 27%; a capital expenditure budget of \$10,200,000; and a headcount plan targeting approximately 450 employees by fiscal year-end. The budget contemplated continued investment in the Company\'s next-generation sensor platform, expansion of the predictive analytics software offering, and increased investment in sales and marketing to support new vertical market penetration.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby approves the FY2024 annual operating budget of the Company, in the form presented to the Board at this meeting, with such modifications as the CEO and CFO may deem appropriate in the ordinary course of business.

Motion by Mr. Garrett, seconded by Mr. Kleinhans. The resolution was unanimously approved (7-0).

**[FY2023 Audited Financial Results]{.underline}**

Ms. Yeung and the representative of Bancroft Keene & Associates LLP presented the Company\'s audited financial statements for fiscal year 2023. Key results included the following: FY2023 revenue of \$127,300,000; Adjusted EBITDA of \$31,800,000, reflecting an Adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 25.0%; and total employees at fiscal year-end of 412. The representative of Bancroft Keene & Associates LLP confirmed that the firm had issued a clean audit opinion with no material weaknesses or significant deficiencies identified in the Company\'s internal controls over financial reporting.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby accepts the FY2023 audited financial statements of the Company as presented by the Chief Financial Officer and the Company\'s independent auditors.

Motion by Mr. Garrett (in his capacity as Chair of the Audit Committee), seconded by Ms. Lau. The resolution was unanimously approved.

**[SensorLink Technologies Acquisition Opportunity]{.underline}**

CEO Ellery presented an opportunity for the Company to acquire SensorLink Technologies, Inc. (\"SensorLink\"), a smaller competitor in the industrial IoT sensor market. SensorLink is a Delaware corporation headquartered in Charlotte, North Carolina. Mr. Ellery described SensorLink as a complementary business with proprietary wireless mesh networking technology that would augment the Company\'s existing sensor platform capabilities, as well as an installed base of approximately 2,000 customers in the discrete manufacturing sector. Mr. Ellery proposed that the Company explore a potential acquisition of 100% of SensorLink\'s equity interests for aggregate consideration of up to \$22,000,000, subject to customary due diligence and negotiation of definitive terms.

Discussion among the directors followed. Mr. Kleinhans expressed support for the strategic rationale, noting that the wireless mesh networking technology could accelerate the Company\'s product roadmap by 12 to 18 months. Ms. Okafor asked about integration risks and competitive dynamics. Mr. Trask asked about the impact on the Company\'s cash position and whether additional debt would be required, noting the recently obtained Clearfield National Bank term loan. Ms. Yeung responded that the Company anticipated funding the acquisition from a combination of existing cash reserves and, potentially, the undrawn capacity under the term loan facility, supplemented by a seller financing component.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby authorizes the Chief Executive Officer to explore a potential partnership with or acquisition of SensorLink Technologies, Inc. for aggregate consideration of up to \$22,000,000, and hereby authorizes the Chief Executive Officer to negotiate and execute a non-binding letter of intent on behalf of the Company, subject to definitive documentation being brought back to the Board for review and approval prior to execution.

Motion by Mr. Kleinhans, seconded by Ms. Chandrasekaran. The resolution was approved (7-0).

**[Other Business and Adjournment]{.underline}**

No other business was raised for discussion. The next regular meeting of the Board was scheduled for April 2024. There being no further business, the meeting was adjourned at 1:00 p.m. Central Time.

Linda Yeung Secretary

**[Meeting 6: Minutes of Regular Meeting of the Board of Directors --- April 17, 2024]{.underline}**

**[Heading and Attendance]{.underline}**

A regular meeting of the Board of Directors of Terravox Systems, Inc. was held on **April 17, 2024**, commencing at **10:00 a.m. Central Time**, at Conference Room A, 4800 Mueller Boulevard, Suite 300, Austin, TX 78723, with the option for attendance via videoconference.

**Directors Present:**

> • Marcus Ellery, Chairman
>
> • Priya Chandrasekaran
>
> • David Kleinhans
>
> • Sandra Okafor
>
> • Rebecca Lau

Five (5) of the seven (7) directors then in office were present, constituting a quorum pursuant to Article III of the Bylaws (majority of 7 = 4).

**Directors Absent:**

> • Julian Trask
>
> • Thomas Garrett

The Secretary noted for the record that Julian Trask had notified the Chairman on April 15, 2024, that he would be unable to attend the meeting due to a scheduling conflict. Mr. Trask did not provide a proxy or written consent regarding any matters on the agenda. Thomas Garrett had notified the Chairman that he would be unavailable due to a family medical matter.

**Also Present:**

> • Linda Yeung, Chief Financial Officer and recording secretary
>
> • Nathaniel Rourke, Esq., Avery, Pollard & Glenn LLP, outside counsel

**Board Observer:** The Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP observer representative was not present at this meeting.

**[Approval of Prior Minutes]{.underline}**

The minutes of the regular meeting of the Board held on January 24, 2024, were presented for review. A motion to approve was made by Mr. Kleinhans and seconded by Ms. Lau. The minutes were approved by the directors present (5-0).

**[Special Committee Report --- Inbound Interest from Hargrove Capital Partners]{.underline}**

Ms. Lau, Chair of the Special Committee, reported that Meridian Partners LLC had received a preliminary inbound indication of interest from Hargrove Capital Partners III, LP (\"Hargrove\"), a private equity fund managed by Hargrove Capital Management, LLC, a Dallas-based private equity firm with approximately \$4.2 billion in assets under management. Ms. Lau stated that Hargrove had expressed interest in a potential acquisition of 100% of the outstanding equity interests of Terravox and had requested access to a virtual data room to conduct preliminary due diligence. Ms. Lau noted that the Special Committee had met on April 10, 2024, to discuss the inbound interest and had determined that the indication was credible and merited further exploration. The Special Committee recommended that the Board authorize the Special Committee to engage with Hargrove and to provide data room access, subject to the execution of a mutually satisfactory confidentiality agreement.

Discussion among the directors present followed. Chairman Ellery noted the significance of the inbound interest and expressed his view that the Board should explore the opportunity in light of the Company\'s strong FY2023 financial results and favorable market position. Mr. Kleinhans indicated that Ridgeline Ventures was generally supportive of exploring a potential sale process, particularly given the current valuation environment for industrial IoT businesses. Ms. Okafor stated that Northbridge Growth Equity Fund IV, LP believed the timing was favorable to engage with a well-capitalized buyer and recommended proceeding. Ms. Lau observed that the Company\'s FY2023 financial results positioned it well for a favorable valuation.

Mr. Trask was not present at the meeting and had not provided a proxy or written direction regarding this matter.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby authorizes the Special Committee to engage with Hargrove Capital Partners III, LP regarding a potential acquisition of the Company, including providing access to a virtual data room containing customary due diligence materials, subject to the execution of a confidentiality agreement in form and substance satisfactory to outside counsel.

Motion by Ms. Lau, seconded by Ms. Okafor. The resolution was approved by the directors present (5-0, with Mr. Trask and Mr. Garrett absent).

**[RSU Grant --- Linda Yeung]{.underline}**

Ms. Lau, in her capacity as Chair of the Compensation Committee, presented a recommendation to grant 200,000 restricted stock units (\"RSUs\") to Linda Yeung, the Company\'s Chief Financial Officer, under the Company\'s 2021 Equity Incentive Plan (as amended). The RSUs would vest over three (3) years, with one-third of the total RSUs vesting on each anniversary of the grant date, subject to Ms. Yeung\'s continued service with the Company. The fair market value of the Company\'s Common Stock was determined to be \$14.30 per share based on the Company\'s most recent independent 409A valuation report. Ms. Lau noted that the grant was consistent with market benchmarks for CFO-level compensation at peer companies and that the Compensation Committee had reviewed the grant in the context of the Company\'s overall equity budget.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby approves the grant of 200,000 restricted stock units to Linda Yeung under the Company\'s 2021 Equity Incentive Plan (as amended), vesting over three (3) years (one-third per year), on terms consistent with the Company\'s standard form of RSU agreement approved for use under the Plan.

Motion by Ms. Lau, seconded by Ms. Chandrasekaran. The resolution was approved by the directors present (5-0).

**[Trask Objection --- Appended Correspondence]{.underline}**

Subsequent to the meeting, on April 19, 2024, the Company received an email from Julian Trask, Director, stating the following (reproduced in relevant part):

> \"I write to register my objection to the Board\'s decision on April 17 to authorize the Special Committee to engage with Hargrove Capital Partners without the full Board\'s consideration and without Stonemill\'s input. Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP, as the holder of 100% of the Company\'s outstanding Series C Preferred Stock, has significant economic and governance rights in connection with any potential change-of-control transaction, and Stonemill\'s perspectives should have been sought before authorizing engagement with a potential acquirer. I request that this objection be noted in the record and that no further substantive steps be taken regarding a potential Hargrove transaction without Stonemill\'s participation.\"

The Chairman acknowledged receipt of the email on April 22, 2024. A copy of the email was appended to the official minutes of the April 17, 2024 meeting and is maintained in the Company\'s minute books as an attachment hereto.

**[Other Business and Adjournment]{.underline}**

No other business was raised for discussion. The next regular meeting of the Board was scheduled for July 2024. There being no further business, the meeting was adjourned at 12:00 p.m. Central Time.

Linda Yeung Secretary

**[Meeting 7: Minutes of Regular Meeting of the Board of Directors --- July 10, 2024]{.underline}**

**[Heading and Attendance]{.underline}**

A regular meeting of the Board of Directors of Terravox Systems, Inc. was held on **July 10, 2024**, commencing at **10:00 a.m. Central Time**, at Conference Room A, 4800 Mueller Boulevard, Suite 300, Austin, TX 78723, with the option for attendance via videoconference.

**Directors Present:**

> • Marcus Ellery, Chairman
>
> • Priya Chandrasekaran
>
> • David Kleinhans
>
> • Sandra Okafor
>
> • Julian Trask
>
> • Rebecca Lau
>
> • Thomas Garrett

All seven (7) directors then in office were present, constituting a quorum pursuant to Article III of the Bylaws.

**Also Present:**

> • Linda Yeung, Chief Financial Officer and recording secretary
>
> • Nathaniel Rourke, Esq., Avery, Pollard & Glenn LLP, outside counsel
>
> • Representatives of Meridian Partners LLC, financial advisor

**Board Observer:** A representative of Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP was present in an observer capacity.

**[Approval of Prior Minutes]{.underline}**

The minutes of the regular meeting of the Board held on April 17, 2024, were presented for review, including the appended correspondence from Julian Trask dated April 19, 2024. Mr. Trask noted for the record that he had reviewed the minutes and confirmed that his objection was properly reflected in the appended correspondence. A motion to approve was made by Ms. Lau and seconded by Mr. Garrett. The minutes were approved 6-1. Mr. Trask abstained from the vote on the minutes, noting his continued objection to the underlying action authorized at that meeting.

**[Special Committee Update --- Hargrove Capital Partners Indication of Interest]{.underline}**

Ms. Lau, Chair of the Special Committee, reported on the status of the Company\'s engagement with Hargrove Capital Partners III, LP. Following the Board\'s authorization at the April 17, 2024 meeting, the Company had executed a confidentiality agreement with Hargrove in form and substance approved by outside counsel, and had provided Hargrove with access to a virtual data room containing customary due diligence materials. Hargrove had conducted preliminary due diligence over a period of approximately ten weeks and had submitted a non-binding indication of interest proposing an acquisition of 100% of the outstanding equity interests of Terravox at an enterprise value range of \$475,000,000 to \$500,000,000.

The proposed transaction structure was a reverse triangular merger, in which Hargrove would form a wholly-owned acquisition subsidiary that would merge with and into Terravox, with Terravox surviving as a wholly-owned subsidiary of Hargrove Capital Partners III, LP. All outstanding equity interests of Terravox would be cancelled and converted into the right to receive merger consideration in cash.

Ms. Lau presented the Meridian Partners analysis of the indication, including the following implied valuation metrics: implied EV/Revenue multiple of approximately 3.7x to 3.9x (based on FY2023 revenue of \$127,300,000); and implied EV/EBITDA multiple of approximately 14.9x to 15.7x (based on FY2023 Adjusted EBITDA of \$31,800,000). Ms. Lau noted that these multiples were within the range of comparable transactions identified by Meridian.

Discussion among the directors followed. Mr. Trask stated that while the valuation range was of interest, Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP reserved all of its rights under the protective provisions of the Third Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation and under the Stockholders\' Agreement, including any consent rights applicable to a change-of-control transaction. Mr. Kleinhans and Ms. Okafor expressed support for advancing discussions with Hargrove. Mr. Ellery and Ms. Chandrasekaran also expressed support for continuing the process.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby authorizes management, acting through the Chief Executive Officer, to enter into an exclusivity agreement with Hargrove Capital Partners III, LP for a period of sixty (60) days, on terms reviewed and approved by the Special Committee and outside counsel.

Motion by Ms. Lau, seconded by Ms. Okafor. The resolution was approved 6-1. Mr. Trask voted against the resolution.

**[Engagement of Valuation Advisor]{.underline}**

The Special Committee recommended the engagement of Thornbury & Associates as an independent valuation advisor to provide a fairness analysis in connection with the potential Hargrove transaction.

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby approves the engagement of Thornbury & Associates as independent valuation advisor to the Board in connection with the potential transaction with Hargrove Capital Partners III, LP, on terms negotiated by the Special Committee.

Motion by Mr. Garrett, seconded by Ms. Lau. The resolution was approved (7-0).

**[Other Business and Adjournment]{.underline}**

No other business was raised for discussion. The next regular meeting of the Board was scheduled for September 2024, with the possibility that a special meeting might be convened earlier if developments with Hargrove warranted Board action. There being no further business, the meeting was adjourned at 1:15 p.m. Central Time.

Linda Yeung Secretary

**[Meeting 8: Minutes of Special Meeting of the Board of Directors --- September 25, 2024]{.underline}**

**[Heading and Attendance]{.underline}**

A special meeting of the Board of Directors of Terravox Systems, Inc. was held on **September 25, 2024**, commencing at **2:00 p.m. Central Time**, at Conference Room A, 4800 Mueller Boulevard, Suite 300, Austin, TX 78723, with the option for attendance via videoconference. This meeting was designated as a Special Meeting of the Board, called by the Chairman pursuant to Article III of the Bylaws, for the purpose of discussing the proposed transaction with Hargrove Capital Partners III, LP and related matters.

**Directors Present:**

> • Marcus Ellery, Chairman
>
> • Priya Chandrasekaran
>
> • David Kleinhans
>
> • Sandra Okafor
>
> • Julian Trask
>
> • Rebecca Lau
>
> • Thomas Garrett

All seven (7) directors then in office were present, constituting a quorum pursuant to Article III of the Bylaws.

**Also Present:**

> • Linda Yeung, Chief Financial Officer and recording secretary
>
> • Nathaniel Rourke, Esq., Avery, Pollard & Glenn LLP, outside counsel
>
> • Representatives of Meridian Partners LLC, financial advisor
>
> • Representatives of Thornbury & Associates, independent valuation advisor

**Board Observer:** A representative of Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP was present in an observer capacity.

**[Approval of Prior Minutes]{.underline}**

The minutes of the regular meeting of the Board held on July 10, 2024, were presented for review. A motion to approve was made by Ms. Okafor and seconded by Ms. Chandrasekaran. The minutes were approved 6-0. Mr. Trask abstained.

**[Hargrove Capital Partners --- Revised Indication of Interest]{.underline}**

Ms. Lau, Chair of the Special Committee, reported that following the 60-day exclusivity period authorized by the Board at its July 10, 2024 meeting, Hargrove Capital Partners III, LP had submitted a revised non-binding indication of interest at a fixed enterprise value of \$485,000,000. The proposed transaction would be structured as a reverse triangular merger, with Hargrove forming a wholly-owned acquisition subsidiary (\"Merger Sub\") that would merge with and into Terravox, with Terravox surviving as a wholly-owned subsidiary of Hargrove Capital Partners III, LP. All outstanding equity interests of Terravox, including all shares of Common Stock, Series A Preferred Stock, Series B Preferred Stock, and Series C Preferred Stock, would be cancelled and converted into the right to receive their respective portions of the aggregate merger consideration, subject to the terms and conditions of the definitive merger agreement.

Meridian Partners presented an updated financial analysis. Thornbury & Associates presented a preliminary valuation assessment. The following key metrics were discussed:

> • Implied EV/Revenue multiple: approximately 3.81x (based on FY2023 revenue of \$127,300,000)
>
> • Implied EV/EBITDA multiple: approximately 15.25x (based on FY2023 Adjusted EBITDA of \$31,800,000)
>
> • Enterprise value of \$485,000,000, less outstanding indebtedness under the Clearfield National Bank term loan facility of \$12,000,000, yielded an implied equity value of \$473,000,000
>
> • On a fully diluted basis of approximately 33,900,000 shares, implied per-share equity value of approximately \$13.95, subject to the waterfall analysis accounting for the liquidation preferences applicable to the Company\'s Preferred Stock
>
> • Total aggregate liquidation preference of the outstanding Preferred Stock: \$206,400,000 (Series C Preferred Stock: \$144,300,000; Series B Preferred Stock: \$48,100,000; Series A Preferred Stock: \$14,000,000)

Discussion among the directors followed. Ms. Lau noted that the implied per-share equity value exceeded the current 409A valuation and that, based on Meridian\'s preliminary waterfall analysis, all holders of Preferred Stock would receive amounts in excess of their respective liquidation preferences on an as-converted basis, suggesting that participation on an as-converted basis would be economically favorable for all preferred holders. Mr. Trask acknowledged that the valuation was within an acceptable range but reiterated that Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP reserved all of its rights and had not yet determined whether it would consent to the proposed transaction.

Following discussion, the following resolution was presented:

**RESOLVED**, that the Board of Directors hereby authorizes management, acting through the Chief Executive Officer, to proceed with the negotiation of definitive documentation with Hargrove Capital Partners III, LP for a proposed acquisition of the Company at an enterprise value of \$485,000,000, structured as a reverse triangular merger, subject to all necessary corporate approvals, Board approval of definitive documentation, and stockholder consents required by applicable law and the Company\'s charter documents.

Motion by Ms. Lau, seconded by Mr. Kleinhans. The resolution was approved 6-1. Mr. Trask voted against, noting for the record that Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP had not yet determined whether it would consent to the transaction and that Stonemill reserved all rights under the Restated Certificate and Stockholders\' Agreement.

**[Stockholder Consent Requirements]{.underline}**

Outside counsel Rourke addressed the Board regarding the stockholder consent and approval requirements that would need to be satisfied in connection with the proposed merger transaction. Mr. Rourke identified the following required consents and approvals:

> \(i\) Approval of the holders of a majority of the outstanding shares of capital stock of the Company, voting together as a single class on an as-converted-to-Common-Stock basis, pursuant to Section 251 of the Delaware General Corporation Law;
>
> \(ii\) Approval of the holders of a majority of the outstanding Preferred Stock, voting together as a single class on an as-converted basis, pursuant to the protective provisions set forth in Article IV of the Third Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation;
>
> \(iii\) Approval of the holders of at least 66⅔% of the outstanding shares of Series C Preferred Stock, voting as a separate class, pursuant to the Series C protective provisions set forth in Article IV of the Third Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation; and
>
> \(iv\) Mr. Rourke noted that the Stockholders\' Agreement dated November 3, 2021, contains drag-along provisions that, if the applicable approval thresholds are met, would obligate all stockholder parties to vote in favor of and participate in a Sale of the Company, as defined in the Stockholders\' Agreement, subject to the thresholds and conditions set forth therein.

Mr. Rourke further noted that the Company\'s Bylaws permit stockholder action by written consent in lieu of a stockholder meeting, which mechanism could be used to obtain the necessary approvals in an efficient manner. Mr. Rourke recommended that the Company work with its outside counsel and Hargrove\'s counsel (Whitfield & Crane LLP) to coordinate the consent solicitation process.

Mr. Trask stated on the record that Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP holds 100% of the outstanding Series C Preferred Stock, consisting of 7,800,000 shares, and that accordingly the Series C separate class vote described in clause (iii) above cannot be satisfied without Stonemill\'s affirmative consent.

**[SensorLink Technologies LOI Status]{.underline}**

CEO Ellery reported on the status of the previously authorized letter of intent with SensorLink Technologies, Inc. Mr. Ellery stated that, following the Board\'s authorization at the January 24, 2024 meeting, a non-binding letter of intent (the \"SensorLink LOI\") had been executed on March 8, 2024. The SensorLink LOI contemplated a potential acquisition of 100% of SensorLink\'s equity interests for aggregate consideration of up to \$22,000,000 and contained a mutual exclusivity provision with an initial term of six months expiring on September 8, 2024.

Mr. Ellery reported that, in light of the Company\'s focus on the Hargrove transaction and certain operational concerns identified during preliminary diligence of SensorLink, the Company had not advanced the SensorLink acquisition and that management believed the matter had concluded. Mr. Ellery stated that the exclusivity period under the SensorLink LOI had expired by its own terms on September 8, 2024, and that the Company was no longer actively pursuing the SensorLink transaction.

In response to a question from Mr. Kleinhans, outside counsel Rourke noted that he had reviewed the SensorLink LOI and observed that the LOI contained an automatic renewal provision with respect to the exclusivity period. Specifically, the exclusivity provision provided that the initial six-month exclusivity period would be automatically extended for successive ninety (90)-day periods unless either party provided written notice of termination to the other party at least fifteen (15) days prior to the expiration of the then-current exclusivity period. Mr. Rourke asked Mr. Ellery whether any such written termination notice had been sent by the Company to SensorLink prior to the September 8, 2024 expiration date.

Mr. Ellery stated that he believed the SensorLink matter had \"run its course\" and that both parties had mutually agreed to move on, but acknowledged that he was not certain whether a formal written termination notice had been delivered in accordance with the terms of the SensorLink LOI. Mr. Rourke recommended that the Company immediately confirm whether a written termination notice was sent prior to September 8, 2024 and, if not, that the Company send one promptly to avoid any ambiguity regarding the Company\'s obligations under the exclusivity provision.

The Chairman directed the CFO and outside counsel to investigate and confirm the status of the SensorLink LOI exclusivity provision and to take any necessary steps to terminate the exclusivity obligation.

**[Other Business and Adjournment]{.underline}**

The Board briefly discussed the anticipated timeline for definitive documentation, noting that Hargrove\'s counsel, Whitfield & Crane LLP, would be commencing comprehensive due diligence in the coming weeks and that a target closing date in Q1 2025 was anticipated. Mr. Rourke stated that his firm would begin preparing the Company\'s disclosure schedules and coordinating with the Special Committee regarding the negotiation of the merger agreement.

No other business was raised for discussion. There being no further business, the meeting was adjourned at 4:30 p.m. Central Time.

Linda Yeung Secretary

**CERTIFICATION**

The foregoing minutes constitute a true and complete record of the proceedings of the Board of Directors of Terravox Systems, Inc. for the meetings held from January 18, 2023 through September 25, 2024, as maintained in the official minute books of the Company at its principal offices at 4800 Mueller Boulevard, Suite 300, Austin, TX 78723. These minutes have been compiled and reviewed by the undersigned in her capacity as Secretary of the Company.

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Linda Yeung Secretary, Terravox Systems, Inc.

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)

"""Validate a .docx for delivery.

Checks ZIP integrity, XML well-formedness for every part, [Content_Types].xml
presence, and relationship consistency (no dangling rIds). Does NOT do XSD
schema validation in v1 — defer until ECMA-376 XSDs are vendored fresh from
ECMA-International.

Usage: python validate.py file.docx
Exit 0 = valid; non-zero = errors printed to stderr.
"""
import sys
import zipfile
from pathlib import Path
from lxml import etree


def validate(path: Path) -> list[str]:
    errors = []

    if not path.exists():
        return [f"File not found: {path}"]
    if not zipfile.is_zipfile(path):
        return [f"Not a valid ZIP: {path}"]

    with zipfile.ZipFile(path) as z:
        names = set(z.namelist())

        if "[Content_Types].xml" not in names:
            errors.append("Missing [Content_Types].xml")
            return errors

        # XML well-formedness for every .xml and .rels part
        for name in names:
            if name.endswith(".xml") or name.endswith(".rels"):
                try:
                    etree.fromstring(z.read(name))
                except etree.XMLSyntaxError as e:
                    errors.append(f"Malformed XML in {name}: {e}")

        # Relationship target consistency

{"file_path": "/workspace/memo-draft.md", "content": "# PRIVILEGED AND CONFIDENTIAL — ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT

**WHITFIELD & CRANE LLP**

610 Lexington Avenue, 38th Floor, New York, NY 10022

---

## GOVERNANCE DILIGENCE MEMORANDUM

**TO:** Elena Marchetti; Jonathan Hsieh
**FROM:** [Associate], Whitfield & Crane LLP
**DATE:** October 28, 2024
**RE:** Governance Diligence — Proposed Acquisition of Terravox Systems, Inc. by Hargrove Capital Partners III, LP
**MATTER:** Hargrove Capital Partners III, LP — Terravox Acquisition

*Privilege Legend: This memorandum constitutes attorney work product prepared in anticipation of litigation and reflects the confidential legal analysis of Whitfield & Crane LLP in connection with the above-captioned matter. It is privileged and confidential, is intended solely for the internal use of Whitfield & Crane LLP and its client, and may not be disclosed to any third party (including the Company, its counsel, or any Company representative) without the prior consent of Jonathan Hsieh or Elena Marchetti.*

---

## I. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

This memorandum summarizes our review of the governance documents of Terravox Systems, Inc. ("Terravox" or the "Company") in connection with the proposed acquisition of 100% of the Company's outstanding equity by Hargrove Capital Partners III, LP ("Hargrove" or "Buyer"), structured as a reverse triangular merger at an enterprise value of $485 million (the "Transaction"). We reviewed the Company's Third Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation (the "Restated Certificate" or "Charter"), Amended and Restated Bylaws (the "Bylaws"), Stockholders' Agreement, Voting Agreement, Registration Rights Agreement (the "RRA"), and Board minutes covering January 2023 through September 2024.

**The central finding of this diligence is that Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP ("Stonemill") holds a hard, charter-level blocking right over the Transaction that cannot be reliably overcome through the governance documents alone.** Specifically, Section 4.3.6(b)(i) of the Restated Certificate requires the affirmative consent of holders of at least sixty-six and two-thirds percent (66⅔%) of the outstanding Series C Preferred Stock, voting as a separate class, to effect any "Liquidation Event" — a threshold that, because Stonemill holds 100% of the outstanding Series C Preferred Stock (7,800,000 shares), cannot be satisfied without Stonemill's affirmative consent. The reverse triangular merger squarely constitutes a "Liquidation Event" under Section 4.3.2(d) of the Charter. This is the decisive leverage point in the deal and the throughline of this memorandum.

**Key conclusions:**

1. **No viable path to closing over Stonemill's objection via the governance documents.** The Series C separate-class protective provision is a non-waivable charter right. The drag-along in the Stockholders' Agreement, even if triggered, most likely cannot override this charter protective provision under Delaware law (see *ODN Holding Corp. 25, LLC v. Knowlton*, Del. Ch. 2011). Accordingly, the Transaction cannot be consummated without Stonemill's consent, and the negotiation strategy must be built around obtaining it.

2. **The all-Preferred protective provision CAN be satisfied without Stonemill.** Section 4.3.6(a)(vi) requires the consent of holders of a majority of the Preferred Stock (voting as a single class on an as-converted basis), a threshold of more than 8,250,000 of the 16,500,000 outstanding Preferred shares. Northbridge (5,200,000) and Ridgeline (3,500,000) together hold 8,700,000 shares, sufficient to satisfy this threshold without Stonemill. This narrows Stonemill's blocking position to the Series C separate-class vote alone.

3. **Stonemill's economic motivation to block is strong.** At $485 million enterprise value (approximately $473 million equity value), the Series C liquidation preference ($144.3 million) consumes a substantial portion of the equity proceeds, and Stonemill would receive essentially its Original Issue Price back with no return, while Series B, Series A, and Common holders capture the upside. This economic structure explains Stonemill's resistance and its likely demand for a higher allocation of consideration or a side arrangement.

4. **A time-sensitive exclusivity issue with SensorLink Technologies may impede signing.** The SensorLink LOI contains an automatic-renewal exclusivity provision; the Company's CEO was unable to confirm whether a termination notice was sent before the September 8, 2024 expiration. If the exclusivity is still live, it could conflict with the Company's ability to sign a definitive agreement with Hargrove. This requires immediate attention.

5. **The $12 million Clearfield National Bank term loan appears to have been incurred without the Preferred Stockholder consent required by Section 4.3.6(a)(v) of the Charter.** The Board minutes reflect only director approval, not the separate stockholder consent the Charter requires for indebtedness exceeding $5 million. This is a governance defect that Stonemill may invoke as leverage.

6. **The Special Committee is not fully independent.** Sandra Okafor, Northbridge's board designee, serves on the three-member Special Committee overseeing the sale process. Her presence undermines the Committee's independence and its cleansing effect, creating a process vulnerability that Stonemill may exploit.

7. **Critical document gaps remain.** We have not received the 2021 Equity Incentive Plan, individual award agreements, the Clearfield loan documents, the SensorLink LOI, or any stockholder consent records. Several conclusions in this memorandum are necessarily preliminary pending production of these items.

A consolidated consent/approval matrix appears in Appendix A. A document request list appears in Appendix B. Severity classifications are summarized in Appendix C.

---

## II. SCOPE, DOCUMENTS REVIEWED, AND CAPITALIZATION

**Documents reviewed.** We reviewed the following documents, each dated November 3, 2021 (except as noted):

1. Third Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation (the "Restated Certificate" or "Charter");
2. Amended and Restated Bylaws (the "Bylaws");
3. Stockholders' Agreement (the "Stockholders' Agreement");
4. Voting Agreement (the "Voting Agreement");
5. Registration Rights Agreement (the "RRA"); and
6. Board minutes for eight meetings spanning January 18, 2023 through September 25, 2024 (the "Board Minutes").

**Transaction structure.** Hargrove will form a wholly-owned acquisition subsidiary ("Merger Sub") that will merge with and into Terravox, with Terravox surviving as a wholly-owned subsidiary of Hargrove. All outstanding equity interests of Terravox (Common Stock and all series of Preferred Stock) will be cancelled and converted into the right to receive cash merger consideration. Enterprise value is $485 million; with approximately $12 million of outstanding indebtedness, implied equity value is approximately $473 million. Target closing is Q1 2025.

**Capitalization (fully diluted, per Stockholders' Agreement Exhibit B).**

| Stockholder | Class | Shares | % (Fully Diluted) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Marcus Ellery | Common Stock | 4,200,000 | ~12.4% |
| Priya Chandrasekaran | Common Stock | 3,100,000 | ~9.1% |
| Ridgeline Ventures Fund II, LP | Series A Preferred | 3,500,000 | ~10.3% |
| Northbridge Growth Equity Fund IV, LP | Series B Preferred | 5,200,000 | ~15.3% |
| Stonemill Capital Partners Fund VI, LP | Series C Preferred | 7,800,000 | ~23.0% |
| Various Common Holders / Option Pool | Common / Options / RSUs | 10,100,000 | ~29.8% |
| **Total (Fully Diluted)** | | **33,900,000** | **100.0%** |

**Board composition (per Voting Agreement § 2.2).** Seven directors: Marcus Ellery (CEO/Chair), Priya Chandrasekaran (Founder designee), David Kleinhans (Ridgeline/Series A designee), Sandra Okafor (Northbridge/Series B designee), Julian Trask (Stonemill/Series C designee), Rebecca Lau (Independent), and Thomas Garrett (Independent). Stonemill also holds a non-voting board observer right (Voting Agreement § 4).

---

## III. AREA 1 — CHANGE-OF-CONTROL CONSENT REQUIREMENTS

To consummate the reverse triangular merger, the following consents and approvals must be obtained. We have mapped each requirement across the Charter, the Stockholders' Agreement, the Voting Agreement, the RRA, and the DGCL.

### A. DGCL Requirements (Section 251)

Under Section 251 of the Delaware General Corporation Law (the "DGCL"), a merger requires (i) approval of the merger agreement by the board of directors of each constituent corporation and (ii) approval by the stockholders of the surviving corporation, unless an exemption applies. The Section 251(f) exemption from stockholder approval is **not available** here because all outstanding shares of Terravox will be cancelled and converted into the right to receive cash — the shares do not "remain outstanding as the same share" as required by Section 251(f)(2). Accordingly, stockholder approval under Section 251(d) is required: the affirmative vote of a majority of the outstanding shares of stock entitled to vote. This is consistent with the analysis of Company counsel (Nathaniel Rourke) reflected in the September 25, 2024 Board minutes.

### B. Charter Protective Provisions

**1. All-Preferred protective provision (Section 4.3.6(a)(vi)).** So long as any Preferred Stock is outstanding, the Company may not "effect any Liquidation Event" without the prior written consent or affirmative vote of holders of at least a majority (greater than 50%) of the then-outstanding Preferred Stock, voting together as a single class on an as-converted basis. The Charter specifies that this requires the affirmative vote or consent of holders of more than **8,250,000 shares** of Preferred Stock (based on 16,500,000 shares outstanding).

- The reverse triangular merger is a "Liquidation Event" under Section 4.3.2(d)(i) (a merger in which the Company's stockholders do not continue to hold at least 50% of the voting power of the surviving entity) and under Section 4.3.2(d)(iii) (a transaction resulting in the transfer of more than 50% of the voting power).
- **This threshold CAN be satisfied without Stonemill.** Northbridge (5,200,000 shares) and Ridgeline (3,500,000 shares) together hold 8,700,000 shares, which exceeds the 8,250,000-share threshold. Stonemill's 7,800,000 shares are not required.

**2. Series C separate-class protective provision (Section 4.3.6(b)(i)).** So long as any Series C Preferred Stock is outstanding, the Company may not "effect any Liquidation Event" without the prior written consent or affirmative vote of holders of at least **66⅔% of the then-outstanding Series C Preferred Stock, voting as a separate class**. The Charter specifies that this requires the affirmative vote or consent of holders of at least **5,200,000 shares** of Series C (based on 7,800,000 shares outstanding).

- **This threshold CANNOT be satisfied without Stonemill.** Stonemill holds 100% of the outstanding Series C Preferred Stock (7,800,000 shares). Julian Trask confirmed this on the record at the September 25, 2024 Board meeting, stating that "the Series C separate class vote ... cannot be satisfied without Stonemill's affirmative consent."
- The Section 4.3.6(b) consent is "separate from and in addition to" the Section 4.3.6(a) consent; both must be independently obtained.

### C. Stockholders' Agreement — Drag-Along (Article 3)

The Stockholders' Agreement contains drag-along rights that, if the "Drag-Along Approval" thresholds are met, would obligate all stockholder parties (including Stonemill) to vote in favor of a Sale of the Company. The drag-along is analyzed in detail in Section IV (Area 2). For purposes of the consent map: the drag-along is not itself a "consent" required to consummate the merger, but it is a mechanism that, if validly triggered and enforceable, could compel dissenting stockholders to vote in favor. As discussed in Section IV, the drag-along most likely cannot override the Series C separate-class protective provision.

### D. Voting Agreement

The Voting Agreement's voting obligations (Section 3) relate to **board composition**, not to the merger itself. Section 3.2 expressly provides that the Voting Agreement "shall not be construed to create any obligation on the part of any Party to vote Shares in any particular manner with respect to any ... proposed merger, consolidation, sale of assets, or other corporate transaction." Accordingly, the Voting Agreement imposes no consent or voting requirement for the Transaction. The Voting Agreement terminates upon a "Sale of the Company" (Section 7.1(b)); see Section VII (Area 5).

### E. Registration Rights Agreement

The RRA does not impose a consent requirement for the merger. It terminates upon a "Deemed Liquidation Event" (Section 9(c)); see Section XI (Area 10) and Section VIII (Area 6) regarding the undefined nature of that term.

### F. Third-Party Consents

- **Clearfield National Bank term loan.** The $12 million secured term loan (authorized October 11, 2023) is secured by substantially all of the Company's assets. The loan agreement (not yet produced) may contain change-of-control covenants requiring lender consent. This must be confirmed. See Section IX (Area 8).
- **SensorLink Technologies LOI.** The exclusivity provision of the SensorLink LOI (executed March 8, 2024) may be live and could impede signing. See Section IX (Area 8).

### G. Consent Checklist

The consolidated consent/approval matrix is set forth in **Appendix A**. In summary, the Transaction requires: (i) Board approval of the definitive merger agreement (not yet obtained — the September 25, 2024 resolution authorized negotiation only); (ii) a majority stockholder vote under DGCL Section 251(d) (not yet obtained); (iii) the all-Preferred consent under Section 4.3.6(a)(vi) (not yet obtained, but achievable without Stonemill); and (iv) the Series C separate-class consent under Section 4.3.6(b)(i) (not yet obtained, and **not achievable without Stonemill**).

---

## IV. AREA 2 — DRAG-ALONG ANALYSIS

### A. The Three-Pronged Trigger

Section 3.1(a) of the Stockholders' Agreement provides that a "Drag-Along Approval" is obtained only upon the satisfaction of **all three** of the following conditions:

1. **Common vote:** holders of at least a majority of the outstanding shares of Common Stock (calculated on an as-converted to Common Stock basis, treating each share of Preferred Stock as converted) (Section 3.1(a)(i));
2. **Preferred vote:** holders of at least a majority of the outstanding shares of Preferred Stock (calculated on an as-converted basis, voting together as a single class) (Section 3.1(a)(ii)); and
3. **Investor vote:** at least two (2) of the three (3) Investor Stockholders (Section 3.1(a)(iii)).

Section 3.1(b) confirms that all three conditions must be satisfied; fewer than all three does not constitute a Drag-Along Approval.

### B. Can the Drag-Along Be Triggered Without Stonemill?

**Condition (ii) — Preferred vote:** The threshold is a majority of the 16,500,000 outstanding Preferred shares (as-converted), i.e., more than 8,250,000 shares. Without Stonemill, Northbridge (5,200,000) and Ridgeline (3,500,000) together hold 8,700,000 shares — sufficient to satisfy this condition. **Satisfiable without Stonemill.**

**Condition (iii) — Investor vote:** At least two of the three Investor Stockholders (Ridgeline, Northbridge, Stonemill) must approve. Without Stonemill, both Ridgeline and Northbridge must approve. **Satisfiable without Stonemill** (assuming both Ridgeline and Northbridge consent, which is consistent with their expressed support in the Board minutes).

**Condition (i) — Common vote:** This condition is the principal uncertainty. The provision requires a majority of the outstanding Common Stock "calculated on an as-converted to Common Stock basis, treating each share of Preferred Stock as having been converted." Read naturally, this is a majority of the fully diluted as-converted base. On a fully diluted basis of approximately 33,900,000 shares, a majority requires more than approximately 16,950,000 shares. The non-Stonemill parties to the Stockholders' Agreement (the Founders, Ridgeline, and Northbridge) collectively hold 16,000,000 as-converted shares (Founders 7,300,000 + Ridgeline 3,500,000 + Northbridge 5,200,000) — approximately 950,000 shares short of the threshold. Satisfaction of this condition without Stonemill would therefore require the support of additional Common holders who are not parties to the Stockholders' Agreement (a portion of the 10,100,000 shares held by "Various Common Holders / Option Pool"). The availability of that support cannot be confirmed without the complete capitalization table and clarification of whether unexercised options and unvested RSUs are counted as "outstanding shares of Common Stock." **Uncertain — likely requires non-party Common holder support.**

**Preliminary conclusion on trigger:** Conditions (ii) and (iii) can be satisfied without Stonemill. Condition (i) is uncertain and may require support from non-party Common holders. Even if all three conditions are satisfied, however, the more fundamental question is whether the drag-along can override the Charter's Series C protective provision, addressed below.

### C. Can the Drag-Along Override the Charter Protective Provisions?

This is the critical legal question. If the drag-along is validly triggered, Section 3.1(a) obligates each Restricted Stockholder — including Stonemill — to vote all of its Shares (including its Series C Preferred Stock) in favor of the Sale of the Company. Section 3.3 reinforces this with an irrevocable proxy granted to the holders constituting the Drag-Along Approval. If Stonemill were compelled to vote 100% of its Series C in favor, the 66⅔% Series C separate-class threshold would be satisfied.

**The difficulty is that the Series C separate-class vote is a protective provision embedded in the Charter — the supreme governance document of the Company.** Under Delaware law, a stockholders' agreement is a contract among stockholders and cannot amend or override the certificate of incorporation. The leading authority is *ODN Holding Corp. 25, LLC v. Knowlton*, 2011 WL 6153080 (Del. Ch. Dec. 2, 2011), in which the Court of Chancery held that a drag-along provision could not be used to circumvent the class-voting protections of the certificate of incorporation. The court reasoned that the certificate of incorporation is the supreme governing document and that a stockholders' agreement cannot be employed to override its protective provisions or to force a transaction that violates a charter class-vote requirement.

There is a colorable counter-argument: the drag-along does not "amend" the Charter but rather compels a stockholder to vote in a particular way, and if the compelled vote satisfies the Charter threshold, no Charter provision is violated. On this view, Stonemill contractually agreed to be dragged and must vote accordingly. The irrevocable proxy in Section 3.3 strengthens the enforcement argument. However, this argument is in tension with the *ODN* reasoning and has not been reliably endorsed by Delaware courts as a means to override a separate-class protective provision to which the protected holder objects.

**Assessment.** This is a genuinely contested question under Delaware law. The weight of authority and the *ODN* line of cases suggest that the drag-along **most likely cannot** override the Series C separate-class protective provision. Relying on the drag-along to close over Stonemill's objection would expose the Transaction to significant litigation risk, including a likely injunction motion by Stonemill seeking to enjoin the closing on the ground that the Series C separate-class consent was not validly obtained. The outcome of such litigation is uncertain and would likely delay or derail the Transaction.

**Recommendation.** Do **not** rely on the drag-along as a primary path to closing over Stonemill's objection. Treat the Series C separate-class vote as a hard blocking right requiring Stonemill's affirmative consent. The drag-along may be retained as a secondary negotiating lever and a potential litigation fallback, but it should not be the cornerstone of the closing strategy.

### D. Drag-Along Terms Affecting Consideration

Section 3.2(a) provides that each Restricted Stockholder receives the same form and amount of consideration per share (as-converted), subject to the liquidation waterfall of the Charter — i.e., consideration is distributed "as if the Sale of the Company were a Liquidation Event." Section 3.2(d) limits stockholder representations and warranties to title, authority, and enforceability, and limits indemnification to a several (not joint) basis capped at the consideration received; no stockholder may be required to enter non-competition or non-solicitation covenants. These terms are favorable to the selling stockholders and would, if the drag-along is invoked, govern the stockholders' obligations in the merger agreement.

---

## V. AREA 3 — IMPEDIMENTS TO CLOSING A REVERSE TRIANGULAR MERGER

### A. Defined Terms Clearly Capture the Structure

The reverse triangular merger is unambiguously captured by the operative defined terms:

- **"Liquidation Event" (Charter § 4.3.2(d)(i)):** "any merger, consolidation, or other business combination of the Corporation with or into another entity (**whether or not the Corporation is the surviving entity**), other than [one in which the stockholders retain at least 50% of the voting power]." The explicit "whether or not the Corporation is the surviving entity" language confirms that both forward and reverse mergers are captured. Because Terravox stockholders will retain 0% of the voting power post-merger (Hargrove will own 100%), the Transaction is a Liquidation Event. Section 4.3.2(d)(iii) (transfer of more than 50% of voting power) is independently satisfied.
- **"Sale of the Company" (Stockholders' Agreement § 1.1; Voting Agreement § 1):** "any merger, consolidation, or reorganization of the Company in which the stockholders of the Company immediately prior to such transaction do not retain a majority of the voting power of the surviving or resulting entity." The Transaction satisfies this definition.

Accordingly, there is **no structural ambiguity** as to whether the reverse triangular merger triggers the Liquidation Event and Sale of the Company definitions. The consent and termination consequences follow.

### B. DGCL Section 251(f) Does Not Apply

As noted in Section III, because all Terravox shares will be cancelled and converted into cash, the Section 251(f) exemption from stockholder approval is unavailable, and a full stockholder vote under Section 251(d) is required. This is consistent with the structure (a 100% cash-out acquisition) and with Company counsel's analysis.

### C. Survival of the Company and Its Contracts

In a reverse triangular merger, Terravox survives as a wholly-owned subsidiary. This has two principal implications:

1. **Contracts generally survive without assignment.** Because the legal entity persists, contracts that would otherwise require counterparty consent upon an assignment or change of control by merger may not be triggered (depending on the specific change-of-control language in each contract). This is generally an advantage of the reverse triangular structure. However, certain contracts contain change-of-control triggers that apply regardless of entity survival — most notably the Clearfield term loan (see Section IX) and potentially the Permian Basin Energy Co. master services agreement and other commercial contracts. We have not yet reviewed these contracts and cannot confirm whether change-of-control consents are required.

2. **Charter and Bylaws survive (as subsidiary documents).** The Charter and Bylaws remain in effect post-closing until amended. The protective provisions become practically moot because no Preferred Stock will remain outstanding post-closing, but they must be satisfied **at closing** (before the shares are cancelled). The Series C separate-class consent is therefore a closing condition, not a post-closing concern.

### D. The RRA "Deemed Liquidation Event" Gap

The RRA terminates upon "a Deemed Liquidation Event" (Section 9(c)), but the RRA does **not define** "Deemed Liquidation Event." The Charter defines "Liquidation Event" (not "Deemed Liquidation Event"), and the Stockholders' Agreement and Voting Agreement define "Sale of the Company." The absence of a defined term in the RRA creates ambiguity as to whether the Transaction terminates the RRA. This is addressed in detail in Sections VIII (Area 6) and XI (Area 10). The merger agreement should include an express termination/waiver of the RRA at closing to eliminate this ambiguity.

### E. Conclusion on Structure

The reverse triangular merger structure does not create unique definitional impediments — the operative defined terms clearly capture it. The principal structure-related risks are (i) the survival of the Company's contracts (requiring review for change-of-control consents, particularly the Clearfield loan) and (ii) the RRA definitional gap. Neither is a deal-breaker, but both require affirmative attention in the merger agreement and diligence.

---

## VI. AREA 4 — TRANSFER RESTRICTIONS

### A. ROFR and Co-Sale (Stockholders' Agreement §§ 2.2–2.3)

Section 2.1 prohibits any Restricted Stockholder from Transferring Shares except (a) in compliance with Article 2, (b) pursuant to a Permitted Transfer (Section 2.7), or (c) "in connection with a Sale of the Company consummated in accordance with Article 3." Section 2.7(e) lists as a Permitted Transfer any "Transfer[] in connection with a Sale of the Company consummated in accordance with Article 3."

The right of first refusal (Section 2.2) is triggered when a Restricted Stockholder "receives a bona fide written offer from a third party ... to purchase any Shares and desires to accept such offer." The co-sale right (Section 2.3) is triggered when the Company and other Restricted Stockholders do not purchase all Shares subject to a ROFR Notice. These provisions are designed for individual stockholder sales to third parties, not for a statutory merger in which all shares are cancelled and converted by operation of law.

**Whether the merger triggers ROFR/co-sale turns on the conditional carve-out.** The Sale of the Company carve-out in Sections 2.1(c) and 2.7(e) is conditioned on the Sale of the Company being "consummated in accordance with Article 3" — i.e., pursuant to the drag-along. If the drag-along is invoked and the Sale of the Company is consummated under Article 3, the merger is a Permitted Transfer and ROFR/co-sale do not apply. If the drag-along is **not** invoked (for example, because the Transaction proceeds on the basis of stockholder consents and a stockholder vote without a formal drag-along trigger), the conditional language creates ambiguity as to whether the merger falls within the carve-out.

**Practical resolution.** The Stockholders' Agreement terminates upon "a Sale of the Company" (Section 6.1(b)), so the transfer restrictions cease upon closing in any event. The concern is whether the restrictions impede the path **to** closing. Because the merger is a statutory transaction (not a voluntary sale by an individual stockholder) and the Stockholders' Agreement terminates upon the Sale of the Company, the better view is that ROFR/co-sale do not apply to the merger. Nonetheless, the conditional carve-out language is imprecise.

**Recommendation.** Obtain an interpretive confirmation or waiver from the stockholder parties (or structure the Transaction to invoke the drag-along formally) to eliminate any argument that ROFR or co-sale rights are triggered. The merger agreement should include a representation that the Transaction constitutes a Sale of the Company and that the transfer restrictions do not apply.

### B. Tag-Along Rights (Section 2.4)

Tag-along rights apply to a "Founder Transfer" — a Founder's transfer of more than 5% of the outstanding Shares to a non-Affiliate third party. The merger is not a Founder Transfer in the ordinary sense; it is a Sale of the Company. The same analysis as ROFR/co-sale applies: the Sale of the Company carve-out and the termination of the Stockholders' Agreement upon a Sale of the Company (Section 6.1(b)) should govern. No separate impediment is identified, but the conditional-carve-out ambiguity noted above applies equally here.

### C. Founder Lock-Up (Section 2.5)

The 12-month lock-up period ran from November 3, 2021 and expired on November 3, 2022. It is no longer in effect and presents no impediment.

### D. Voting Agreement Transfer Restriction (Section 6.2)

Section 6.2 of the Voting Agreement prohibits Transfers unless the transferee agrees to be bound by the Voting Agreement. The Voting Agreement terminates upon a Sale of the Company (Section 7.1(b)), so this restriction ceases upon closing. The merger is not a voluntary Transfer by a party in the conventional sense. No impediment is identified.

### E. Conclusion on Transfer Restrictions

The transfer restrictions should not impede the Transaction, particularly given that the Stockholders' Agreement and Voting Agreement both terminate upon a Sale of the Company. The principal risk is the conditional carve-out language ("consummated in accordance with Article 3"), which creates ambiguity if the drag-along is not formally invoked. We recommend addressing this through waiver/confirmation or formal drag-along invocation, and through appropriate provisions in the merger agreement.

## VII. AREA 5 — BOARD COMPOSITION: POST-CLOSING IMPLICATIONS

### A. Termination of the Voting Agreement Upon the Merger

Section 7.1 of the Voting Agreement provides that the agreement terminates upon the earliest of: (a) an IPO; (b) "the consummation of a Sale of the Company (as defined in Section 1)"; (c) written consent of the Company and specified stockholder majorities; or (d) November 3, 2026 (the fifth anniversary).

The "Sale of the Company" definition (Section 1) captures the reverse triangular merger (a merger in which the stockholders do not retain a majority of the voting power of the surviving entity). Accordingly, **the Voting Agreement terminates upon closing of the Transaction**. The board designation rights in Section 2.2 lapse at closing; they do not survive, and the November 3, 2026 sunset is moot if the Transaction closes before that date (target Q1 2025).

**Post-closing board.** Upon closing, Hargrove, as the sole stockholder of the surviving subsidiary, will control the board and may appoint directors as it sees fit. No designation rights, voting obligations, or committee-composition requirements under the Voting Agreement survive. This is a clean outcome.

### B. Stonemill's Board Observer Right (Section 4)

Section 4.1 grants Stonemill the right to appoint a non-voting observer to attend all Board (and committee) meetings and to receive all Board materials, for so long as Stonemill holds at least 1,000,000 shares of Series C Preferred Stock (or Common issued upon conversion). Section 7.3 provides that the observer right terminates if Stonemill ceases to hold the minimum share threshold.

Upon closing, all Series C Preferred Stock is cancelled and converted into cash, so Stonemill ceases to hold any Series C (or Common issued upon conversion). The observer right therefore terminates at closing. In addition, the Voting Agreement itself terminates upon the Sale of the Company (Section 7.1(b)), independently terminating the observer right. **The observer right does not survive the Transaction.**

**During the signing-to-closing period, however, the observer right is live.** Stonemill's observer is entitled to attend Board meetings and receive Board materials during the pendency of the Transaction. This is significant because the Board will be considering the merger agreement and related matters. Section 4.2 permits the Company to exclude the observer (and withhold materials) to preserve attorney-client privilege or to address a bona fide conflict of interest between Stonemill and the Company, upon advice of counsel. Given Stonemill's adversarial posture, the Company should be prepared to invoke this exclusion judiciously for privileged deliberations and conflict-sensitive matters. We note that the Stonemill observer was **not present** at the April 17, 2024 meeting at which the Board authorized engagement with Hargrove — a fact relevant to the sale-process analysis in Section X (Area 9).

### C. Committee Composition (Section 2.4)

Section 2.4 provides that, unless otherwise agreed by the Investor Stockholders, each Board committee must include at least one Investor Stockholder designee. This requirement is live during the signing-to-closing period. The Special Committee currently includes Sandra Okafor (Northbridge's designee), satisfying this requirement. Post-closing, the requirement is moot. The composition of the Special Committee is analyzed in Section X (Area 9).

### D. Conclusion on Board Composition

The Voting Agreement and its designation rights and observer rights terminate cleanly upon closing. No post-closing governance entanglement arises from the Voting Agreement. The principal operational concern is managing Stonemill's observer rights (and information access) during the signing-to-closing period.
